codeReview.summary: Full 4-level autonomy enforcement wired correctly. TaskType enum implements strategic/routine classification. AutonomyOverrideMatrix supports per-agent-type and per-task-type overrides with correct priority resolution (specific combo > agent > task > global > base). needs_approval() correctly enforces: Supervised (always approval), Guided (strategic only), Autonomous (high-risk only + strategic if override), Full Auto (never unless strategic override). All types properly exported from lib.rs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The autonomy module uses a builder pattern for AutonomyOverrideMatrix (add_override, add_agent_override, add_task_override, set_global_fallback). These methods consume self and return Self, which is idiomatic but worth documenting if there's a convention for builder patterns in this codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: autonomy.rs: AutonomyOverrideMatrix methods all use `mut self` and return `Self` for chaining

summary: Feature orch-autonomy-enforcement passes review. Implementation provides full 4-level autonomy enforcement (Supervised/Guided/Autonomous/FullAuto) with per-agent-type and per-task-type override matrix. TaskType enum classifies work as strategic or routine. 27 autonomy tests pass. Clippy clean. No hardcoded test doubles in production paths.
